APPALLING WALLACE


REPORT

pages.raw_text:
Unless this is cut within the next
year it will entail loss of enor.
nous proportions to the govern.
ment and private owners.


This morning Supervisor Weigle
sent Ranger A. E. Holcomb to open
up the old Mullan road leading to
the Bullion mine, where eight
bodies are buried. These will be
brought to Wallace "Wednesday
morning. Ten soldiers from Fort
George Wright left at o'clock to
aid in clearing the path.


A crew was also sent out to the
Big Fork of the Occur d'Alene
where IL bodies are buried under
the ruins. This will necessitate
the claring of 80 mills of road tC
Wallace.


Besides the IL dead at Big Fork
three men are lying with broker
legs and three totally blinded. DR.
Max T. Smith of Wallace is head
ing the relief expedition.


KNOWN LIST OF DEAD.


Lou Holmes. Spokane.


Tom welsh, spokane.


George Ziegler, Newport, 81
years old.
Mis-. Ernest Demhardt, Newport
George Campbell, Newport. -
Thomas Carey, Newport.


Cady, rancher. Newport
Henry Herman, Garwood, Idaho
V. Nicholson, age II. Gem. Idaho
Larry Ryson, age so, Wallace.


Leslie Sellers, age Is, Gem
Idaho.


S. d. Adams, age 80, Chicago.


A. Renston, Hillsdale, Wis.

William Mickey, Taft, dead al
Saltese.

Creek.

Creek.


Joseph c. Boyd, Wallace.


Joe Fene, Placer Creek.


William Hearmouth, War Eigle
mine.


Joe Smith, burned near Mullan.


"Frenchy." familiar Spokane
character, killed on Placer creek.


John Address. wife and five chill
dren, La Tour creek, near Cataldo
reported dead.


b. A. Smith, wife and two chill
dren, La Tour creek, near Cataldo
